mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment growth signals impending economic weakness, causing risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 100.0 thousand
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in housing starts and consumer credit outstanding reflects a synchronized withdrawal of household spending, which compresses corporate revenue expectations and depresses S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 60% of co-occurring contraction episodes, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds its historical trend while inflation remains anchored, supply-side capacity constraints translate into higher profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO exceeds 105 and CPIAUCSL year-over-year change remains below 2.5%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the aggregate demand for corporate goods and services, directly boosting earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The quarterly GDP revision trend shows no correlation with 5-day forward returns, or the Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 0.5%, the market prices in a tightening monetary stance that increases borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The index produces positive forward returns in more than 55% of instances following the rate crossing, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for S&P 500 dividend yields.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where payroll employment rises but the unemployment rate simultaneously increases indicates a surge in labor force participation outpacing job creation, signaling weakening labor market tightness and depressing S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 60% of observed instances, or the joint effect disappears when controlling for wage growth proxies.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid housing market expansion, rising home values stimulate consumer wealth and spending, which directly lifts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when HOUST exceeds 1100 units, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% when mortgage rates rise above 4.0%.
